note_data.title: Chords of a Circle
keyPoints: A chord is a line segment that joins any two points on a circle
The diameter is the longest chord of a circle passing through the center
Equal chords of a circle are equidistant from the center
The perpendicular from the center of a circle to a chord bisects the chord
Equal chords subtend equal angles at the center of the circle
If two chords intersect inside a circle, the product of their segments are equal
The angle in a semicircle is always a right angle (90°)
Chords equidistant from the center of a circle are equal in length

definitions.definition: A chord is a straight line segment whose endpoints both lie on the circle
A diameter is a chord that passes through the center of the circle and is the longest possible chord
A perpendicular bisector of a chord is a line that passes through the center of the circle and is perpendicular to the chord, dividing it into two equal parts
An arc is a portion of the circumference of a circle between two points on the circle
A segment is the region of a circle bounded by a chord and the arc subtended by the chord

importantQuestions.question: State the theorem about perpendicular from center to chord.
importantQuestions.answer: The perpendicular from the center of a circle to a chord bisects the chord. Also, the line joining the center to the midpoint of a chord is perpendicular to the chord.

importantQuestions.question: In a circle with center O, two chords AB and CD intersect at point P inside the circle. If AP = 6 cm, PB = 4 cm, and CP = 8 cm, find PD. Also prove the theorem used.
importantQuestions.answer: Given: AP = 6 cm, PB = 4 cm, CP = 8 cm. By the intersecting chords theorem: AP × PB = CP × PD. Therefore: 6 × 4 = 8 × PD, 24 = 8 × PD, PD = 3 cm. Theorem: When two chords intersect inside a circle, the product of the segments of one chord equals the product of the segments of the other chord.

mcqs.question: The longest chord of a circle is:
If two chords of a circle are equal, then they are:
The angle in a semicircle is:

commonMistakes: Students confuse chord with diameter - remember diameter is a special chord passing through center
Forgetting to state the theorem before applying it in proofs
Not drawing proper diagrams with clear labels for center O and chord points
Mixing up the intersecting chords theorem with other circle theorems
Not mentioning that perpendicular from center bisects the chord AND the arc
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question worth 3-5 marks in every BSEK paper
Always draw a neat circle diagram with center O clearly marked
Memorize the exact statement of perpendicular bisector theorem - appears every year
Practice numerical problems on intersecting chords - very common in long questions
Remember to write 'Hence Proved' at the end of theorem proofs
formulas: For intersecting chords: AP × PB = CP × PD
Distance from center to chord: d = √(r² - (l/2)²) where r = radius, l = chord length
Chord length: l = 2√(r² - d²) where d = distance from center
note_data.keyDiagram: Draw a circle with center O. Show two chords AB and CD intersecting at point P inside the circle. Mark the perpendicular from O to any chord, showing it bisects the chord. This basic diagram covers most theorem applications in this chapter.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (4 marks) — asked students to define chord and state the perpendicular bisector theorem
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q6 (6 marks) — required proof that perpendicular from center bisects the chord
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q3 (3 marks) — asked to differentiate between chord and diameter with examples
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q5 (5 marks) — asked to prove equal chords are equidistant from center

workedExample.question: Define chord of a circle. Prove that the perpendicular drawn from the center of a circle to a chord bisects the chord. (6 marks)
steps: Definition: A chord is a straight line segment that joins any two points on the circumference of a circle
Draw circle with center O, chord AB, and perpendicular OM to AB meeting AB at M
In triangles OMA and OMB: OA = OB (radii), OM = OM (common), angle OMA = angle OMB = 90°
Therefore triangles OMA ≅ OMB by RHS congruence
Hence AM = MB, so M is the midpoint of AB
Therefore perpendicular from center bisects the chord
workedExample.answer: A chord is a straight line segment joining any two points on circumference. Proof shows perpendicular from center creates two congruent right triangles, making the foot of perpendicular the midpoint of chord.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for textbook definition, 1 mark for diagram, 2 marks for identifying congruent triangles, 1 mark for stating congruence rule, 1 mark for conclusion

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing 'chord is a line in circle' instead of the textbook-exact definition 'straight line segment joining two points on circumference'
Always draw accurate diagrams with proper labeling - many students lose marks for unclear or unlabeled geometric diagrams
When proving chord theorems, state the congruence rule clearly (RHS, SAS, etc.) - simply saying 'triangles are equal' gets no marks
note_data.quickRecap: Chord is a straight line segment joining two points on circumference. Diameter is the longest chord passing through center. Perpendicular from center bisects any chord. Equal chords are equidistant from center.
